skill.name: configuring-dapr-pubsub
skill.description: Guides configuration of Dapr pub/sub components for event-driven microservices using Kafka or Redis. It covers components, subscriptions, publishing patterns, sidecar deployment, local development, and troubleshooting.
skill.summary: Configure Dapr pub/sub with Kafka or Redis for event-driven microservices.
skill.author: Asmayaseen

security_audit.summary: Static blocker and command-execution detections are mostly false positives from Markdown code examples and harmless verifier text. No malicious intent, exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The main concern is that some Kafka examples disable authentication or TLS, which could lead to insecure deployments if copied into production.

medium_findings.title: Insecure Kafka Authentication and TLS Examples
medium_findings.description: The quick-start Kafka component sets authType to none and disableTls to true. The section labeled Kafka production also uses authType none. These are not malicious, but users could copy them into environments that require authenticated and encrypted broker traffic.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The configuration values are directly visible and weaken transport security. The risk depends on deployment context, so this is a publishable documentation concern rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur inside Markdown fenced examples for kubectl, dapr, curl, Docker Compose, YAML, and Python snippets. The skill does not execute these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced documentation blocks and troubleshooting commands. No subprocess, shell, eval, or backtick execution was found in the verifier script.

low_findings.title: Local Dapr HTTP Examples Require Deployment Review
low_findings.description: The Python examples post data to the local Dapr sidecar and include user identifiers, session identifiers, and generated responses. This is expected for Dapr pub/sub, but teams should review privacy and topic access controls before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network target is localhost and consistent with Dapr sidecar use. The concern is operational data handling, not evidence of external exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Verbose Dapr API Logging Example
low_findings.description: The deployment example enables Dapr API logging. This can help troubleshooting, but production deployments should avoid logging sensitive request metadata unnecessarily.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The setting is directly present in a deployment example. The impact is context-dependent because logged content depends on Dapr configuration and workload behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unauthenticated Kafka Pub/Sub Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples show Kafka pub/sub with authType none and disabled TLS. Use SASL, secret references, and TLS for shared or production broker networks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The insecure values are explicit and repeated. A secure SASL example is also present, which lowers the likelihood of malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: False Positive Static Blocker Patterns
dangerous_patterns.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts do not correspond to malicious behavior in context. The verifier only reads SKILL.md and checks required text, and the marked SKILL.md lines are documentation or normal sample code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines show local validation logic and documentation examples, not reconnaissance or cryptographic implementation. No evidence of prompt injection, persistence, credential access, or data exfiltration was found.

content.value_statement: Event-driven services need consistent pub/sub wiring across local and Kubernetes environments. This skill provides Dapr component, subscription, publishing, and deployment patterns for Kafka or Redis.
actual_capabilities: Provides Dapr component YAML examples for Kafka and Redis pub/sub backends.
Shows declarative and FastAPI programmatic subscription patterns.
Demonstrates event publishing through the Dapr HTTP sidecar.
Includes Kubernetes deployment annotations for Dapr sidecar injection.
Covers local Dapr CLI, Docker Compose, and troubleshooting commands.
Shows a multi-agent routing pattern using topics and CloudEvents.
limitations: It does not provision Kafka, Redis, Kubernetes, or Dapr control plane resources.
Security settings must be adapted before production use.
Examples may need updates for specific Dapr component versions.
It does not implement full application business logic.

use_cases.title: Set up service messaging
use_cases.description: Create Dapr pub/sub components and subscriptions for services that exchange events through Kafka or Redis.

use_cases.title: Prepare Kubernetes deployment manifests
use_cases.description: Add Dapr sidecar annotations and component scoping to application deployments.

use_cases.title: Diagnose event delivery issues
use_cases.description: Use sidecar metadata, logs, topics, and component settings to isolate pub/sub configuration problems.

prompt_templates.title: Create a local Redis setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a simple Dapr pub/sub setup using Redis for two local FastAPI services.

prompt_templates.title: Configure Kafka pub/sub
prompt_templates.prompt: Design Dapr Kafka pub/sub components and subscriptions for three microservices with separate topics.

prompt_templates.title: Secure a production component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Dapr Kafka pub/sub configuration and recommend authentication, TLS, secret, and scope changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan multi-agent routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Dapr pub/sub routing pattern for agents that publish requests, responses, and failure events.

output_examples.input: I need Dapr pub/sub between a triage service and specialist services.
output: A topic plan for request and response events.
A subscription approach for each service.
Deployment notes for Dapr sidecars and component scopes.

output_examples.input: Review my Kafka component before production.
output: A security checklist covering authentication, TLS, secret references, and broker endpoints.
A list of unsafe defaults to replace.
Operational notes for retries, consumer groups, and logging.

output_examples.input: My subscriber is not receiving events.
output: A troubleshooting sequence for sidecar health, metadata, topics, routes, and logs.
Likely causes grouped by component, subscription, and application routing.
Next checks for namespace, app ID, and resource path mismatches.

best_practices: Use secret references for broker passwords and avoid plain text credentials.
Scope pub/sub components to only the applications that need them.
Enable authentication and TLS for shared or production brokers.
anti_patterns: Do not copy development settings with disabled TLS into production.
Do not publish sensitive user data without topic access controls.
Do not rely on direct broker clients when the architecture expects Dapr abstraction.

faq.question: Does this skill install Dapr?
faq.answer: No. It provides configuration and usage patterns after Dapr is available.

faq.question: Can it work with Kafka and Redis?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes examples for Kafka and Redis Dapr pub/sub components.

faq.question: Is it suitable for production?
faq.answer: It can guide production work, but authentication, TLS, secrets, and scopes require review.

faq.question: Does it support Kubernetes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Dapr sidecar annotations, component scoping, and deployment examples.

faq.question: Does it generate application code?
faq.answer: It provides FastAPI examples and patterns, but it does not build a complete service.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but some examples show insecure Kafka settings that need warnings.
